description: A vertical designed graphic combining a U.S. flag motif with a photographic background. The composition layers a worn American flag painted/projected onto wooden plank slats (red, white, and blue stripes with white stars on a blue field) and superimposes the dark silhouettes of several people - appearing to be a group including adults and possibly children - standing in shadow, conveying anonymous migrants. In the upper-left corner is the standard DHS/USCIS campaign banner: a solid blue box with white serif text reading 'Making America Safe Again'. Across the lower third, large bold white sans-serif text delivers the core message in two parts: a headline 'If you're here illegally, leave now.' followed by 'Return the right way and respect our laws for a chance to live the American dream.' The color palette is patriotic red, white, and blue with darkened, ominous shadowing over the human figures. The messaging technique is a self-deportation appeal blending threat ('leave now') with a conditional promise of legitimacy/reward ('the American dream'), targeting undocumented immigrants and signaling the administration's enforcement-plus-encouragement posture to the broader public. Designed agency graphic.
notable_text: Making America Safe Again. If you're here illegally, leave now. Return the right way and respect our laws for a chance to live the American dream.
